input.background: An early publication from the Framingham Heart Study identified 51 incidences of coronary disease among 424 men with high serum cholesterol (245 mg per 100 mL and above) over a 6-year period. Among 454 men with lower cholesterol (less than 210 mg per 100 mL), there were 16 incident cases.
input.question: Calculate the proportion of coronary disease incidences among men with high serum cholesterol (245 mg per 100 mL and above).
